message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker — close br-3hsb, br-hk3v; add br-10wc TUI epic

Close br-3hsb (conformance fixture parity) and br-hk3v (scripts/am +
/api<->/mcp aliasing). Add br-10wc epic for AgentMailTUI: full
frankentui-native interactive operations console.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-179","title":"MCP Agent Mail Rust: 100% feature parity + conformance + benchmarks","description":"Goal: complete the Rust port of legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code with 100% behavior/feature coverage, proven by fixture-based conformance tests + benchmarks (similar standard as rich_rust/beads_rust).

Non-negotiables:
- Prefer local crates over upstream ecosystem where applicable:
  - MCP: /dp/fastmcp_rust
  - SQLite: /dp/sqlmodel_rust
  - IO/concurrency: /dp/asupersync (avoid tokio unless unavoidable)
  - Console UI: /dp/frankentui
  - Beads: /dp/beads_rust
  - Agent detection: /dp/coding_agent_session_search
- No destructive commands or file deletions without explicit permission.

","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:04:26.956492167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:38:40.435532507Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:05:57.078301787Z","close_reason":"Superseded by br-2ei (correct prefix + clean description)","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-02-05T05:38:40.435526636Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"bad prefix legacy epic; superseded by br-2ei","original_type":"epic","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-10wc","title":"[epic] AgentMailTUI: full frankentui-native interactive operations console","description":"## Background\nPrevious work delivered a rich startup banner and inline HUD in the HTTP server. The next step is a true standalone TUI experience (showcase quality) that makes mcp_agent_mail not found in ~/mcp_agent_mail feel like a first-class operations cockpit, not just decorated logs.\n\n## Vision\nBuild AgentMailTUI as a real multi-pane app inspired by  showcase patterns: smooth navigation, dense but legible telemetry, live event flow, searchable history, and direct operational actions.\n\n## Product Goals\n- Make mcp_agent_mail not found in ~/mcp_agent_mail the easiest and most compelling way to run, observe, and operate MCP Agent Mail.\n- Consolidate message activity, tool calls, reservations, contact status, and system health in one interactive surface.\n- Support both MCP and API transport modes with seamless operator ergonomics.\n- Default to polished, information-rich UX while preserving power-user controls and performance.\n\n## Non-Goals\n- Do not remove existing server/web functionality.\n- Do not ship novelty visuals that hurt clarity, accessibility, or throughput.\n- Do not regress testability or deterministic output in CI/PTY tests.\n\n## Architecture Direction\n- Reuse and extend existing frankentui integration rather than inventing a parallel UI stack.\n- Introduce a typed event stream model feeding dashboard/search/inspect panes.\n- Use bounded buffers, incremental query paths, and virtualized rendering for sustained responsiveness.\n\n## Required Quality Bar\n- Comprehensive unit tests + PTY/E2E scenarios with detailed structured logging artifacts.\n- Clear dependency layering across beads so parallel implementation is safe.\n- Documentation/runbooks sufficient for new contributors to ship changes without tribal context.\n\n## Deliverable\nA complete, staged implementation plan (children of this epic) covering architecture, UX, data flow, connector modes (MCP/API), performance, testing, rollout, and docs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:12:19.615580143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:12:19.615580143Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-10wc","title":"[epic] AgentMailTUI: full frankentui-native interactive operations console","description":"## Background
The current rich console output still feels like decorated logs, not a true operations cockpit. We need a **real full-screen AgentMailTUI** inspired by `/dp/frankentui` demo showcase patterns (dashboard, timeline, search, command palette, deterministic UX + testability).

## Hard Product Contract (User-Mandated)
1. Typing `am` must launch **both**:
   - MCP Agent Mail server, and
   - the full interactive AgentMailTUI
   in one default flow.
2. This must work with either MCP or API base paths (e.g. `/mcp/` or `/api/`).
3. Rich TUI experience is enabled by default; non-TUI fallback remains explicit and intentional.
4. Console layout must be interactively configurable (placement/ratio/height etc.) and persisted automatically in Agent Mail config.

## Vision
Deliver a showcase-grade, operator-first TUI where the main surface is live activity (requests/messages/tool calls), plus instantly accessible search, timeline inspection, health/status signals, and high-leverage controls.

## UX Outcomes
- Fast comprehension: operators instantly see system state + high-signal events.
- Fast navigation: search + palette + keyboard shortcuts for every major action.
- Fast diagnosis: inspect event context, errors, and suggested remediation in-place.
- Fast customization: live layout tuning that auto-persists and restores on startup.

## Architecture Direction
- Reuse frankentui patterns/components from `ftui-demo-showcase` (dashboard, log/virtualized search, action timeline, command palette, layout inspector).
- Add a typed AgentMail event stream feeding all panes consistently.
- Keep render/update loops deterministic and testable (unit + PTY + golden snapshots + e2e logging).
- Use bounded buffers and virtualized lists for sustained throughput.

## Quality Bar
- Comprehensive unit tests for reducers/parsers/layout/state.
- PTY/e2e tests for launch flow, navigation, search, timeline, layout persistence, transport toggles.
- Detailed structured logs/artifacts to debug failures quickly.
- Perf budgets and no-regression checks for interactive responsiveness.

## Deliverable
A full bead graph under this epic with explicit dependencies covering:
- product/UX spec,
- architecture + data/event pipeline,
- launcher orchestration (`am` one-command),
- dashboard/search/timeline/layout/palette surfaces,
- MCP/API mode integration,
- tests/perf/docs and rollout gates.","acceptance_criteria":"- `am` starts server + full AgentMailTUI together by default.
- Operator can switch/use MCP or API mode without restarting flow complexity.
- TUI default experience materially improves clarity over current rich logs.
- Layout tuning is interactive and persisted/restored automatically.
- Unit + PTY/e2e suites cover the critical user journeys with detailed artifacts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:12:19.615580143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:16:55.086281720Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-10wc.1","title":"[track] Product contract and showcase UX spec for AgentMailTUI","description":"Define explicit UX/behavior contract for AgentMailTUI, grounded in frankentui showcase patterns and user-mandated am one-command flow.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:17:01.916174022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:17:01.916174022Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-10wc.1","depends_on_id":"br-10wc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:17:01.916174022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-10wc.1.1","title":"Capability matrix from frankentui showcase to AgentMailTUI","description":"Map concrete showcase features (dashboard, action timeline, log/virtualized search, command palette, layout controls, perf HUD) to AgentMailTUI requirements. Include explicit include/exclude rationale and accretive sequencing.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.640342365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.640342365Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-10wc.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-10wc.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.640342365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-10wc.1.2","title":"Operator journey specification and information architecture","description":"Define primary and secondary user journeys: startup, observe, search, inspect, remediate, configure layout. Produce pane-level IA and navigation rules that minimize context switching and maximize situational awareness.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.738159501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.738159501Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-10wc.1.2","depends_on_id":"br-10wc.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.738159501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-10wc.1.3","title":"Default-on UX acceptance gates including am contract","description":"Formalize acceptance criteria: am launches server+tui by default, MCP/API compatibility, interactive layout persistence, low-noise defaults, and operable fallback modes. These gates block implementation completion until met.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.839432278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.839432278Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-10wc.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-10wc.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.839432278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-10wc.1.4","title":"Regression boundaries versus legacy rich console behavior","description":"Capture what must remain available from current console output while moving to full TUI. Define explicit non-regression checklist so quality improves without losing useful diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.944463892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.944463892Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-10wc.1.4","depends_on_id":"br-10wc.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:28.944463892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Add verbosity tiers, filters, and severity highlighting so default output is high signal and non-overwhelming while preserving depth on demand.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:02.837197447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:02.837197447Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-10wc.11","depends_on_id":"br-10wc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:02.837197447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"br-10wc.3.3","title":"Transport/path selection logic for MCP and API endpoints","description":"Implement and validate endpoint selection so launch flow works cleanly with /mcp/ and /api/ base paths, including explicit overrides and sensible defaults.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:29.672206166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:29.672206166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-10wc.3.3","depends_on_id":"br-10wc.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:18:29.672206166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1bm","title":"HTTP Server Parity: Legacy Python Exact Match","description":"## Objective
Port the **HTTP server stack** to Rust with exact legacy Python behavior: auth, rate limiting, streamable HTTP transport, mail UI endpoints, logging, and background workers.

## Scope
- Middleware: bearer auth, JWT + RBAC, rate limiting.
- Streamable HTTP MCP transport (stateless ASGI adapter behavior, header normalization, base path mounting).
- Mail UI routes and static assets.
- Request logging + OTEL no‑op parity.
- Background workers (ack TTL, file reservation cleanup, tool metrics, retention/quota).
- Health + well‑known endpoints.

## Tests
- Unit + integration tests per subsystem (JWT, rate limit, streamable HTTP, mail UI, logging, workers).
- E2E HTTP suite (br-2ei.9.6) covering auth, CORS, streamable, and UI endpoints.

## Logging/Artifacts
- HTTP transcripts + diffs under `tests/artifacts/http/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:32:32.057076856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646906905Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646884493Z","close_reason":"All HTTP server parity child beads complete (auth/JWT/RBAC, rate limit, peer addr/localhost bypass, streamable HTTP, /mail SSR UI, CORS, logging+OTEL no-op, workers, health/well-known)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:48:14.330942304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-36w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:23.374551664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.1","title":"JWT Auth Parity (HS256 + JWKS + role claim)","description":"Objective:
Implement JWT authentication exactly as in legacy Python (mcp_agent_mail/http.py). This includes HS256 HMAC and JWKS verification paths, audience/issuer validation, role extraction, and error semantics. Must be 1:1 on outputs and failure modes.

Legacy Reference Behavior (must match):
- If JWT enabled and Authorization missing or not Bearer: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Decode token using:
  - If JWKS URL set: fetch JWKS, select key by header.kid, verify
  - Else if secret set: verify with HMAC secret
  - Algorithms from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default HS256)
- Validate audience/issuer if configured; otherwise ignore
- On any failure: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Extract roles from claim HTTP_JWT_ROLE_CLAIM (default "role")
  - String -> singleton set
  - List/tuple -> set of strings
  - Else -> empty
- If roles empty, use HTTP_RBAC_DEFAULT_ROLE
- Store claims for rate limit identity (sub) and any downstream uses

Implementation Considerations:
- JWKS fetch must be async-safe via asupersync HTTP client (no tokio)
- Cache JWKS by URL with TTL to avoid per-request latency
- Must not change external response schema or status codes
- Log auth failures at debug/trace (no extra HTTP output)

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
Unit tests:
- Missing Authorization header -> 401
- Non-Bearer Authorization -> 401
- HS256 valid/invalid signatures
- JWKS valid/invalid (kid missing, bad signature)
- Audience/issuer mismatch -> 401
- Role claim variants (string, list, missing)
Integration tests:
- Start server with HTTP_JWT_* env vars; make requests with valid/invalid JWT
- Verify RBAC path uses roles correctly
E2E:
- Covered by `br-1bm.1.5` (uses shared harness `br-2ei.9.1`).

Notes:
- Localhost bypass logic is owned by `br-1bm.3` + `br-1bm.8`; JWT must still match legacy in the non-bypass path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JWT behavior matches legacy for all vectors extracted in `br-1bm.1.1`.
- Role extraction matches Python behavior across all supported claim shapes.
- `br-1bm.1.5` unit+integration+E2E suite passes and produces high-signal artifacts on failure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperBarn","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:32:46.671284098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T13:29:42.957613837Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T13:29:42.957588740Z","close_reason":"All subtasks br-1bm.1.1-1.5 complete; JWT unit+integration+E2E coverage passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.1.1","title":"JWT Parity: extract legacy behavior + test vectors","description":"Purpose:
Extract exact legacy JWT behavior and define concrete test vectors so Rust matches Python 1:1.

Legacy Implementation Notes (from http.py):
- Header parse: token.split(".",1)[0]; base64url decode with padding; JSON parse. Any error -> None.
- If jwks_url set:
  - httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5) GET jwks_url -> .json()
  - JsonWebKey.import_key_set(jwks)
  - kid = header.get("kid"); key = find_by_kid(kid) if present else key_set.keys[0]
- Else if secret set:
  - JsonWebKey.import_key(secret, {"kty":"oct"})
- If key None -> Unauthorized.
- jwt.decode(token, key) using algs from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default ["HS256"]). Any error -> Unauthorized.
- If audience configured: claims.validate_aud(audience)
- If issuer configured: if str(claims.get("iss") or "") != issuer -> Unauthorized (no fallback)
- claims.validate() (handles exp/nbf/iat if present)
- Returns dict(claims) on success.

Concrete Test Vectors (must implement):
1) Missing Authorization header -> 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
2) Authorization not Bearer -> 401
3) Header parse fail (malformed base64 / non-JSON header) -> 401
4) JWKS path:
   - jwks_url set, kid present, matching key -> success
   - jwks_url set, kid present, no matching key -> 401
   - jwks_url set, kid missing -> uses first key in key_set
   - jwks fetch fails / invalid JSON -> 401
5) Secret path:
   - jwt_secret set, HS256 valid -> success
   - jwt_secret set, signature invalid -> 401
6) Algorithms:
   - alg not in HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS -> 401
7) Audience:
   - jwt_audience set, token aud matches -> success
   - jwt_audience set, token aud mismatch -> 401
8) Issuer:
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss matches exactly -> success
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss missing or different -> 401
9) Claims validate:
   - exp in past -> 401
   - nbf in future -> 401
   - exp/nbf absent -> ok
10) Role claim:
   - role claim string -> roles={string}
   - role claim list -> roles={strings}
   - role claim missing/invalid -> roles empty -> default role applied downstream

Deliverable:
- A markdown or JSON matrix of inputs -> expected HTTP status/body and roles extracted.
- Must be explicit enough that Rust tests can be derived directly.

Dependencies: none (spec extraction only).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:09.570324403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060998320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060980056Z","close_reason":"Extracted legacy JWT behavior + explicit test vectors in bead description","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
